comment: Placeholder produced by the perf(dof) atomic commit (drop 8 MiB depth copy in favour of std::span<const float>). The micro-benchmark that overwrites this file lives at tests/renderer/camera/test_per_pixel_dof.cpp::PerPixelDOF: 1920x1080 micro-benchmark - drop depth copy (delta report). Run with: ./build-fast.sh test "*1920x1080*" in any CMake 3.27+ environment. The bench reports both `after_ms` (kernel-only with span direct, zero copy) and `before_alloc_copy_ms` (simulated wasted copy cost) so the per-frame delta is observable on the CI host. The commit author intentionally did not commit fabricated measurements -- this file documents the bench contract instead.

expected_measurements.after_ms: kernel-only path (span direct, zero copy). Fill in from CI run.
expected_measurements.before_alloc_copy_ms: isolated wasted copy cost = std::vector<float>(depth.begin(), depth.end()) copy + dealloc, 8 MiB per call. Fill in from CI run.
expected_measurements.before_total_ms: after_ms + before_alloc_copy_ms (sum; the kernel work is identical in both paths so this is the legacy BEFORE-path total cost).
expected_measurements.delta_per_frame_ms: before_alloc_copy_ms (the wasted copy time saved -- the perf(dof) atomic commit drops this exact amount).

writer_lookup_chain: compile-time macro CHRONON3D_SOURCE_DIR (set by tests/renderer_tests.cmake via target_compile_definitions)
walk-up from cwd looking for CMakeLists.txt
